user_query:
I'm coordinating a tiny remote team on a short tribute to Ollie Green and need a tidy weekly sync pack everyone can trust. Please work only with the local files in input/ and produce the outputs below under output/.

Inputs to use:
- input/tasks.csv
- input/standup_notes.jsonl
- input/review_comments.md
- input/week_context.yaml
- input/draft_messages.md

Deliverables:
1) output/extracted_review_comments.json
   - Extract structured review items from input/review_comments.md.
   - Only include lines containing a [SHOT:XXXX] tag.
   - For each item, output fields: shot_id (e.g., "S010"), priority ("high|medium|low" if present, else "unspecified"), note (the feedback text without the [SHOT:...] tag or trailing punctuation), frames_range (object with integer start and end if the text contains "frames X-Y" or "frame N"; use {"start":N,"end":N} for single frame; otherwise null).

2) output/rollup_by_shot.json
   - Summarize work per shot_id found in input/tasks.csv.
   - For each shot_id, include: shot_id, total_tasks, status_counts (object mapping each status to its count), owners (unique owner names sorted A–Z), overdue_count, blocked_count, review_comments (count of items for that shot from output/extracted_review_comments.json), standup_blockers (unique list of blocker strings from input/standup_notes.jsonl that mention the shot_id).
   - Definitions:
     • open task = status != "Done"
     • overdue = task due_date < report_week_end from input/week_context.yaml AND status != "Done"
     • blocked_count = number of tasks with status == "Blocked"

3) output/tasks_overdue.csv
   - A CSV of all tasks from input/tasks.csv that are overdue by the above definition.
   - Columns: task_id, title, owner, due_date, status, shot_id.

4) output/weekly_sync_report.md
   - Compose a clear, team-friendly weekly status for our sync. Structure it with these sections:
     • Summary Metrics: total_tasks (all tasks), open_tasks (status != Done), overdue_count, blocked_count — ensure these numbers are consistent with output/rollup_by_shot.json.
     • Per Shot Highlights: for each shot_id present in tasks.csv, 1–2 concise bullets that reflect status_counts and any matching review comments (by priority) from output/extracted_review_comments.json.
     • Risks & Blockers: list per-shot risks derived from standup_blockers and any tasks with status == "Blocked".
     • Next Steps: one actionable line per shot informed by the above.
   - Keep it factual and aligned with the data you computed.

5) output/rewritten_messages.md
   - Rewrite both drafts in input/draft_messages.md for tone, clarity, and brevity:
     • Provide two labeled items: "Slack post for #animation" and "Weekly email draft".
     • Each must be <= 120 words, friendly, inclusive, and action-oriented.
     • The content must not contradict the metrics in output/rollup_by_shot.json or the overdue list.

Notes:
- Use input/week_context.yaml to determine what is overdue.
- Use only local files; do not contact external services.
- All outputs must be written to the exact paths specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
